axis_responsibility: Brick: owns template return requirements and comparison observations for repository-artifact grounding.
Agent: owns tool policy refs, performer receipt, and closed returned facts; Agent does not classify outcome or choose Movement.
Link: owns Movement and gate sufficiency only; this design does not change Link vocabulary or route policy.
Support: renders adapter instructions, validates tier safety, and records/checks evidence; support must fail closed and must not become authority.
candidate_file_changes: support/connection/agent_adapter.py: add/use closed tier resolver and provider-specific read-tier rendering text.
support/checkers/profiles or profile fixtures: add packet rendering and tier safety pins if existing profile structure supports this path.
brick/templates/bricks/code-attack-qa/brick.md: require inspected repository artifact references in return evidence.
brick/templates/bricks/axis-attack-qa/brick.md: require inspected repository artifact references in return evidence.
brick/templates/bricks/evidence-integrity/brick.md: require inspected repository artifact references in return evidence.
brick/templates/bricks/review/brick.md: require inspected repository artifact references in return evidence.
brick/templates/bricks/design/brick.md: require inspected repository artifact references when design work reviews live repo artifacts.
comparison/checker fixture surface: add insufficient case for packet-only review return if current comparison code lacks this observation.
checker_or_verifier_plan: Packet-rendering pin: fixture a reviewer-readonly non-write request and assert read-tier rules render; mutate renderer back to binary and observe RED.
Tier safety pin: fixture non-write read-tier request and assert no Edit/Write/file-mutation/git-mutation permissions render; mutate renderer to grant write language and observe RED.
Grounding pin: fixture QA/review return with no repository artifact references and assert comparison reports missing grounding facts; packet-only return is insufficient.
Adapter mapping pin: codex-local and claude-local read tier render only admitted read operations; gemini-local remains none if no safe read-only expression exists.
Regression command requested by task: PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 PYTHONPATH=support/import_identity python3 support/checkers/check_profile.py --all.
returned.design_summary: Design the repair as an explicit tool-access tier resolver used by adapter instruction rendering: write tier remains existing effective-write behavior; read tier admits read-only repository inspection for non-write reviewer/coordination/design work; none tier remains the fail-closed default. Add artifact-grounding requirements to review/design Brick templates so returns must cite inspected repo artifacts before Brick comparison can observe required facts.
edge_cases: A write-capable lead on a no-write Brick step should receive read tier, not write tier, when effective_write is false.
A reviewer-readonly policy with an adapter that cannot express read-only tools should receive none tier.
A return citing only task packet text, model reasoning, or uninspected claims should be insufficient for grounding.
A checker command that writes cache files must be run with no bytecode/write-safe environment or excluded from read tier wording.
Malformed evidence references should not be silently accepted as repository artifact grounding.
invariants: Non-write read tier must never render edit/write permissions.
Unknown policy vocabulary, missing policy refs, ambiguous adapter capability, or unsupported provider mapping must resolve to none tier.
Read tier may inspect artifacts but may not mutate files, git state, hooks, SDK state, credentials, sessions, network, or provider runtime.
Artifact-grounding requirement must be expressed as required public evidence, not as quality approval or reviewer judgment.
Checker green, model review, or rendered packet text remain support evidence only.

Whether claude-local currently supports checker-command wording beyond Read/Grep/Glob is not proven.
Whether gemini-local has any safe read-only tool expression is not proven.
Current comparison implementation hook point for artifact-grounding insufficiency is not proven.
No FIRE RED/GREEN execution was performed in this design return.
No source truth, success judgment, quality judgment, or Movement authority is claimed.

Inline AGENTS constraints require support not to choose Movement, create GateFacts, judge quality/success, or open runtime/scheduler behavior.
No live repository inspection was performed because this design packet forbids tools.

proposed_changes: Introduce a closed internal tier decision: write, read, none. Ambiguity maps to none.
Keep write tier unchanged: only effective write may render file-edit permissions.

Admit read tier for non-write requests where tool_policy_refs include reviewer-readonly or leader-coordination, and for no-write lead/design requests where read-write-scoped is present but effective_write is false. This is read permission only, not mutation authority.

Codex-local read tier rules should permit file reads, searches, diffs, and checker command execution under read-only sandbox; explicitly forbid edits, git mutation, hooks/SDKs, external network, secrets, and credential/session access.
Claude-local read tier should render a plan/permission mode limited to Read, Grep, and Glob, plus any existing read-only checker invocation language if supported by the adapter text.
Gemini-local should remain none tier unless its policy surface can express read-only tools without ambiguity; document this as a limit rather than widening behavior.
Update review/design Brick contracts to require evidence_used or equivalent required evidence to include inspected repository artifact references such as file paths or diff hunk refs.
Comparison should treat returns with only packet references and no repository artifact references as insufficient for the grounding requirement.
relevant_current_structure: support/connection/agent_adapter.py owns provider instruction rendering and should contain or call the tier resolver.
Agent tool policies provide vocabulary: reviewer-readonly, leader-coordination, and read-write-scoped.
Brick templates under brick/templates/bricks/code-attack-qa, axis-attack-qa, evidence-integrity, review, and design define required return evidence contracts.
Comparison behavior should observe missing repository-artifact references as missing required facts, without adding judgment vocabulary.
